messages.empty: Translate the next German word in `translation.en`.
messages.paraphraseLoss: Paraphrases still strip lexicalization, Gefühl register, Spitzen valence, and lineage. Try the source surface instead.
messages.pendingAdmission: `Schadenfreude` preserves the surface, but this lesson still blocks it until `PersonalVocabulary.pkl` admits the loanword.
messages.vocabularyMissing: Add `Schadenfreude` to `entries` as a `LoanwordEntry` before this translation can pass.
messages.vocabularyShape: Finish the `LoanwordEntry` block so `PersonalVocabulary.pkl` can admit `Schadenfreude`.

translation.title: Peek: LoanwordRules.pkl
translation.explanation: Lesson 2 keeps the same four structural-loss axes. Switching to the preserved surface avoids paraphrase, but does not yet satisfy vocabulary admission.

vocabulary.title: Peek: PersonalVocabulary.pkl
vocabulary.explanation: Lesson 2 adds a vocabulary gate: the preserved surface still fails until `entries["Schadenfreude"]` exists as a `LoanwordEntry`.

completion.title: loanword(Schadenfreude) -> admitted
completion.message: you solved this by amending vocabulary, not by refining the gloss

lossAxes.name: lexicalization
lossAxes.title: lexicalization
lossAxes.message: Malicious joy lexicalizes a summary and drops the portable loanword surface.

lossAxes.name: gefuhl_register
lossAxes.title: Gefühl register
lossAxes.message: The felt social charge survives more densely in Schadenfreude than in an English paraphrase.

lossAxes.name: spitzen_valence
lossAxes.title: Spitzen valence
lossAxes.message: The arc keeps this axis name: the word's sharpened social edge is blunted when English explains instead of preserves.

lossAxes.name: lineage
lossAxes.title: lineage
lossAxes.message: The loanword's literary and cultural lineage disappears when the term becomes a descriptive gloss.
